name: bim-regulation-rs1
description: Jurisdictional regulatory overlay panel. Renders a static constraint lookup table for the BC RS-1 residential zoning overlay (BC Building Code 2018 + NBC 2020 Part 11). Read-only reference surface — NOT an interactive constraint engine. First implementation of the Doctrine claim #41 (City Code as Composable Geometry) pattern. General shell is bim-regulation-overlay; this file is the specific RS-1 overlay instance.
stub_source: design-component-bim-regulation-rs1.draft.md — project-bim 2026-05-06. Operator decision 2026-05-07: recipe.html format (portable HTML contract, not render.rs Rust-specific).
html:
BC, Canada — RS-1 Residential BC Building Code 2018 + NBC 2020 Pt.11 In development — v0.0.3
Regulatory constraints for BC RS-1 Residential
Element Constraint Required value Unit Source
IfcWall (IsExternal=true) ThermalTransmittance ≤ 0.210 W/m²K NBC 2020 §9.36
IfcSlab (IsExternal=true) ThermalTransmittance ≤ 0.183 W/m²K NBC 2020 §9.36
IfcWindow ThermalTransmittance ≤ 1.40 W/m²K NBC 2020 §9.36
IfcDoor (IsExternal=true) ThermalTransmittance ≤ 1.40 W/m²K NBC 2020 §9.36
IfcWall (IsExternal=true) Min. height from grade 150 mm BC Building Code §9.13
Three-file bundle
  • woodfine-bc-rs1.ids — IDS 1.0 constraint specification
  • woodfine-bc-rs1.ifc — IFC exclusion-zone fragments
  • bsdd-woodfine-bc.json — bSDD dictionary

Priority: municipal (1) → provincial (2) → federal (3) → accessibility (4)

Numeric: effective_value = max(regulation_min, climate_zone_min)

Geometric: exclusion-zone IFC fragments take unconditional precedence

three_layer_composition.geometric_rule: exclusion-zone IFC fragments take unconditional precedence

aria: <section> with descriptive aria-label. Status chip: aria-label='Status: [value]'. Table: <caption> 'Regulatory constraints for [jurisdiction]'. <th scope='col'> for column headers. Source abbreviations: <abbr title='[full citation]'>. <details>/<summary> for three-file bundle.
service_dependency: service-codes (port 9103): GET /overlays/{jurisdiction-id} → { constraints: [...], ids_url, ifc_url }. At v0.0.1 service-codes is scaffold-coded; overlay data is hardcoded in the recipe until v0.0.3 endpoint ships.
doctrine_reference: Doctrine claim #41 — City Code as Composable Geometry
